name: clang-21
title: clang-21
description:
C, C++ and Objective-C compiler
Clang project is a C, C++, Objective C and Objective C++ front-end
based on the LLVM compiler. Its goal is to offer a replacement to the
GNU Compiler Collection (GCC).
.
Clang implements all of the ISO C++ 1998, 11, 14 and 17 standards and also
provides most of the support of C++20.

arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for clang++-21.
arguments.raw: -21  Option present in package usage line for clang++-21.

commands.name: clang-21
commands.description: the Clang C, C++, and Objective-C compiler

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use clang++-21 because C, C++ and Objective-C compiler.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use clang++-21 as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `clang++-21 [options] [args]` as documented upstream for clang++-21.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair clang++-21 with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `clang++-21 --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for clang++-21.
